methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $7.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Stegra is a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Carl-Erik Lagercrantz and Harald Mix that has raised $7.0B+ to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes 19 peer companies across public and private markets (1 public, 8 private comparable companies identified), including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or similar-tier density to support a reliable peer-median valuation approach.

Given data constraints, the Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using total raised ($7.0B) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Growth-stage step-up multiplier of 1.2x yields an implied valuation of $8.4B. This reflects the progression pattern observed across single-stage advancement in the venture capital continuum.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $7.1B to $9.7B, with a median point estimate of $8.4B. This range reflects approximately ±15% sensitivity around the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrowly comparable public or late-stage private transaction multiples in the Climate & Energy sector, the reliance on historical stage-progression multipliers rather than peer-derived multiples, and incomplete visibility into Stegra's unit economics and revenue trajectory relative to the broader peer universe.
